message:
fix(engine): make heap allocation fallible and fix deterministic serde length/data mismatch

Two targeted safety improvements:

1. baseline_interpreter.rs — alloc_object_with_prototype returns Result:
   Previously used `unwrap_or(u32::MAX)` which silently saturated,
   causing ObjectId aliasing when heap exceeded u32::MAX objects. Now
   returns `Err(InterpreterError::TypeError)` with a clear message.
   Also hardens closure_id allocation with `u32::try_from().map_err()`
   instead of `as u32` cast. Both Construct call sites updated to
   propagate the error with `?`.

2. deterministic_serde.rs — clamp encoded data to match length prefix:
   When encoding Bytes/String/Array/Map values whose length exceeds
   u32::MAX, the old code wrote `u32::MAX` as the length prefix but
   still wrote ALL the data. On decode, the cursor would advance by
   u32::MAX bytes and land mid-value, corrupting every subsequent
   field. Now clamps both the prefix AND the data/iteration to the
   same `min(len, u32::MAX)` value, preserving roundtrip correctness.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
